threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Stokey' instead of the proper 'Stoke Newington'—the locals will definitely notice and smirk.
Caught a late 59 the other night; a guy was singing to himself so loudly, even the driver had to smile. Brixton after dark is something else.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a guy rapping in a mix of German and Turkish on the corner while a stray cat lounged on a fancy café's terrace. Berlin magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you have spontaneous techno parties in random warehouses, where the smoke is thick and the music doesn't stop for 48 hours.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the tacos at La Taqueria taste even better after a dive at the Elbo Room. Tourists just hit the spots on Yelp.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing a violin on the 31-Balboa—one hand gripping his case, the other bowing like he’s performing at the SF Symphony. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but locals know the best tapas spots are hidden in the alleys, away from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they struggle with the metro ticket machines – staring intensely like it's a Rubik's cube.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep looking for a parking space like it’s a normal day, not a Saturday in Dalston when every car’s a battle.
Caught a lad on the 55 last night singing to his reflection in the window. Whole bus just watched—some laughed, others pretended to be asleep. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy at the corner bakery arguing with the baker over a forgotten croissant, it hit me—this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous 'silent discos' in the parks, where folks dance with headphones on, completely lost in their own vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can decode the BART delays like it’s second nature, while the tourists are just looking around confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy trying to sell a hot dog during a Muni delay at Powell, claiming it was '95% more authentic than the ones at Fisherman’s Wharf.' Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think locals hang out in the squares all day, but we actually avoid them during siesta—too many loud visitors in the afternoons.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they stand in the middle of the street, phone in hand, staring blankly at a map instead of just looking up.
